title: Discovery of novel A2AR antagonist via 3D-QSAR pharmacophore modeling: neuroprotective effects in 6-OHDA-induced SH-SY5Y cells and haloperidol-induced…
impact: Identified a novel A2A receptor antagonist with potential neuroprotective properties.
problem: There is a continuous need for highly specific A2AR antagonists to treat neurodegenerative and motor conditions with fewer side effects.
method: 3D-QSAR pharmacophore modeling combined with in vitro validation in 6-OHDA-induced SH-SY5Y cells and haloperidol-induced models.
results: Successfully discovered and validated a novel antagonist showing significant neuroprotective efficacy against specific induced toxicities.
impact_long: Integrates computational modeling with biological validation to accelerate the discovery of targeted therapies for Parkinson's and other motor disorders.

title: BBPT attenuated 6-OHDA-induced toxicity by modulating oxidative stress, apoptotic, and inflammatory proteins in primary neurons and rat models of Parkinson's disease
impact: Validated the neuroprotective efficacy of BBPT against Parkinsonian neurotoxicity.
problem: Finding effective interventions capable of halting the cascade of oxidative stress and apoptosis in Parkinson's disease progression.
method: In vitro evaluation using primary neurons and in vivo studies in rat models using 6-OHDA to induce Parkinson's-like toxicity, followed by BBPT treatment.
results: BBPT successfully modulated inflammatory, apoptotic, and oxidative stress pathways to mitigate 6-OHDA-induced cellular and neurological damage.
impact_long: Highlights BBPT as a promising multi-target therapeutic candidate for neuroprotection and disease modification in Parkinson's disease.

title: Glutamate uptake is not impaired by hypoxia in a culture model of human fetal neural stem cell-derived astrocytes
impact: Demonstrated the resilience of fetal neural stem cell-derived astrocytes under hypoxic conditions.
problem: Understanding how oxygen deprivation (hypoxia) affects glutamate transport in the developing brain is crucial for addressing ischemic injuries.
method: Analysis of in vitro culture models of human fetal neural stem cell-derived astrocytes subjected to induced hypoxic conditions.
results: Revealed that fundamental glutamate uptake mechanisms remain remarkably functional despite the presence of hypoxic stress.
impact_long: Provides foundational insights into the inherent neuroprotective capacities of developing astrocytes, highly relevant for neonatal hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy.

title: Human fetal neural stem cell-derived astrocytes maintain glutamate transport after hypoxic injury in vitro
impact: Preprint establishing early foundational data for astrocyte resilience under hypoxia.
problem: Lack of preliminary data regarding how fetal astrocytes regulate glutamate during early-stage hypoxic injury.
method: In vitro assays measuring glutamate transport post-hypoxic injury in NSC-derived astrocytes.
results: Initial findings showing sustained glutamate transport functionality despite the simulated hypoxic environment.
impact_long: Served as the early-access precursor study to peer-reviewed findings, accelerating knowledge dissemination regarding fetal astrocyte behavior.
